event: substantial smoking history of greater than 40 pack years
spiculated suprahilar 4.5 × 2.6 cm right upper lobe (RUL) nodule on a screening low-dose CT scan
multiple sub-centimeter left lung nodules consistent with lung-RADS 4X
positron emission tomography/CT (PET/CT) scan showed augmented activity in the RUL mass
bronchoscopy-attained tissue sample showed that the RUL mass was a NSCLC, consistent with adenocarcinoma
lymph node sampling was negative for nodal involvement, consistent with stage IIIA lung adenocarcinoma (T4N0M0)
surgical resection of the mass was planned
surgical resection aborted due to encasement of the pulmonary artery and superior vena cava
encasement of the superior vena cava
completed 7 rounds of carboplatin and paclitaxel
repeat CT scan revealed a decrease in the RUL mass from 4.5 × 2.6 cm to 3.5 × 2.1 cm
premature discontinuation of durvalumab due to pneumonitis
steroid taper prescribed and completed for pneumonitis
staging chest CT showed an increase in the RUL mass to 5.0 × 2.8 cm
follow-up chest CT showed an increase in the RUL mass to 6.5 × 2.2 cm without distant metastasis
CT scan in September 2021 showed no distant metastasis and relatively stable lung nodules
CT scans between September 2021 and June 2022 showed stable appearance of the lung nodules and post-radiation changes consistent with radiation fibrosis
CT scan in June 2022 showed stable disease
abdominal and pelvic CT scan showed a mass in the head of the pancreas measuring 3.1 × 3.4 cm and pancreatic ductal dilatation to 7 mm
referred to gastroenterology for endoscopic ultrasound-guided biopsy of the pancreatic head mass
endoscopic ultrasound-guided biopsy of pancreatic head mass performed
pancreatic mass biopsy showed metastasis from lung adenocarcinoma
PET/CT scan showed fluorodeoxyglucose-avid mass in the pancreatic head with SUV max of 13.3 and increased uptake in the porta hepatis with SUV max of 4.2
no other regions of metastases appreciated
brain MRI scan was negative for intracranial metastasis
palliative radiation therapy to the pancreatic mass
combination chemotherapy with carboplatin and pemetrexed initiated
chemotherapy held due to hospitalization for generalized weakness and dyspnea at rest
acute anemia with hemoglobin nadir of 6.9 g/dL
CT scan revealed a new 9 mm left lower lobe nodule
staging PET/CT scan showed decreased metabolic activity in the pancreatic mass and metabolic activity in the new left lower lobe nodule
patient expressed uncertainty regarding treatment continuation due to fatigue
no further treatments given after third chemotherapy cycle
saturating at 87% on 3 L oxygen via nasal cannula
imaging revealed right lung consolidation and loculated pleural effusion
acute hypoxic respiratory failure due to sepsis secondary to pneumonia
oxygen supplementation with bilevel-positive airway pressure
patient passed peacefully under comfort measures
